chapter_context.summary: A glorious hymn of praise exalting God for bringing down city fortresses, protecting the poor, making a feast for all people, and swallowing up death in victory.

outline.text: Praise to God for Overthrowing Fortress of the Oppressor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Praise to God for Overthrowing Fortress of the Oppressor (25:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Great Messianic Feast and the Swallowing Up of Death
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Great Messianic Feast and the Swallowing Up of Death (25:6-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Trust of the Faithful and Humbling of Moab's Pride
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Trust of the Faithful and Humbling of Moab's Pride (25:9-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 25, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
